groups.context_description: Term names the inner quality of compassion as a defining human characteristic — its violation by circumstances of extreme suffering marking the depth of the catastrophe
Term names God's steadfast love as the defining inner character of his covenantal disposition toward his people — the loyal, enduring, freely given love that grounds all relationship with him
Term names the human inner disposition of covenantal loyalty and loving-kindness — the settled character quality of faithfulness in relationship
Term describes the human appeal to and meditation on God's steadfast love as a sustaining inner orientation — the soul's trust and hope grounded in God's chesed
Term names the responsive quality of inner covenantal mercy — the faithfulness of character that draws out corresponding faithfulness from God
Term names divine compassion as the inward movement of mercy that acts on behalf of those in need, despite their unworthiness
Term names God's inner disposition of grace freely expressed toward persons — the divine movement of favour toward the undeserving
Term names the human inner act of seeking grace — the disposition of humble appeal directed toward God or another from a place of need and dependence
Term designates the inner character of graciousness — the settled disposition of the person (primarily God) to extend favour and mercy freely to those who need it
Term names almsgiving as the outward expression of an inward disposition of mercy and generosity — the inner orientation of compassion made visible in action
Term names the pitiable inner condition — the state of the person who is spiritually destitute, whether through false confidence or loss of hope
Term describes pity and compassion as an inner disposition that moves toward or spares those in need — whether in a person or in God
Term names the withholding of pity as a judicial inner disposition — the inner setting aside of compassionate impulse in the service of righteous judgment
Term names sympathy as a character disposition of the inner person — the settled quality of being responsive to the suffering of others
Term names the inner experience of shared suffering — the disposition and capacity to genuinely participate in another's pain as an expression of union and solidarity
Term names the inner capacity to moderate one's emotional response toward those who are weak or wayward — a controlled, empathetic gentleness arising from shared vulnerability
Term names the deep, intensely felt compassion of God as a character attribute — the inner quality of being profoundly moved toward those who suffer
Term is used as the vehicle of a maternal metaphor grounding divine compassion in the image of carrying from the womb — the most intimate care expressed through the body's innermost space
Term names the visceral, inward movement of compassion — the inner person being deeply stirred and moved toward one who suffers or is in need
Term names the divine compassion as an inner disposition of sovereign mercy — God's free inclination toward the objects of his compassion
Term names the compassionate inner movement of comfort — the tender, stirring compassion that arises from within the person toward those who grieve or suffer
Term names the inner capacity for fellow-feeling — the disposition to enter into and share the experience of another's suffering
The ark as the visible sign of God's covenant presence and faithfulness
The ark as means of preservation amid judgment — framing inner-being contrast of obedience and heedlessness
Faithlessness as an inner-being character quality — covenant-breaking disposition
Covenant promise of unbroken continuation — no lack of faithful persons in God's covenantal line
Divine covenant initiated by God — the relational framework of his commitment
Covenant made before God with all-heart commitment
Prohibited covenant — binding to false loyalties
Covenant of intimate personal loyalty — friendship and inner-being commitment between persons
Legal dissolution of the marriage covenant — formal relational rupture
Divorce as metaphor for God's covenantal rupture with Israel — inner-being severity of apostasy
Divine oath as expression of God's inner commitment to his covenant promise
Human oath as the binding of inner will and loyalty
Swearing by God's name — oath as expression of inner-being allegiance or its corruption
Adjuration — invoking in the context of intense inner longing and love
Prophetic weeks as the divinely appointed schedule for covenant fulfillment
Weeks as the measure of inner-being mourning and self-denial
God's covenant with creation and the patriarchs — unconditional divine inner commitment
The Sinai/Mosaic covenant — bilateral covenant of law and obedience
The new covenant — inner transformation through God's law written on the heart
The Davidic covenant — everlasting covenant of steadfast love and kingship
Covenant as bond of relational loyalty between persons — friendship, marriage, and inner moral integrity
Covenant as God's relational bond through Christ's blood
New covenant written on hearts — inner transformation
Covenantal standing — identity defined by inclusion or exclusion
The covenantal cutting ritual — self-imprecatory act underlying oath
Cutting as moral consequence — cutting off of wickedness
Oath as solemn inner-being binding commitment
Covenant curse as consequence of inner moral failure
Firm covenant — the inner-being act of solemn communal commitment
New covenant — unprecedented inner transformation through Spirit
New self / new creation — inner-being renewal of the person in Christ
New name, song, Jerusalem — eschatological inner-being identity, worship, and hope
Term names the participant in a shared inner-spiritual reality — one who shares in divine nature, suffering, comfort, or cultic alignment through inner disposition and orientation
Term names the participant in a moral or relational bond — one whose inner orientation places them in solidarity or complicity with another person or cause

Term names the inner-relational orientation of shared life and communion with God — the spiritual participation in the divine nature, the Son, and the Spirit that constitutes the believer's vertical fellowship
Term names the inner-relational bond of mutual belonging, shared commitment, and generous participation among believers — the horizontal fellowship that arises from shared life in Christ
Term names the deliberate inner act of leaving — forsaking possessions, family, or prior obligations for the sake of discipleship — as the outward expression of an inner reordering of priorities
Term names the abandonment of what is owed — leaving a spouse, neglecting the weightier matters of the law, or forsaking first love — as an inner failure of loyalty and relational commitment
Term names the relational priority inherent in the leave/stay act — leaving a gift to seek reconciliation first, leaving the many for the one, the Father's not leaving the Son alone
Term names God's act of releasing a person from the guilt and penalty of sin — the vertical forgiveness that restores the inner standing of the person before God
Term names the human act of forgiving another — the horizontal release of an offender from the inner claim of the offended, commanded as the pattern for those who have received divine forgiveness
Term names the act of permitting or granting access as an inner-being posture — the receptivity, welcome, or restraint that reflects an inner orientation of priority, reverence, or obedience

Term names the withholding of permission as an act reflecting the inner-being condition of the actor — whether as spiritual authority, obstruction of others, or the inner abandonment of proper orientation
Term names God's act of forgiving sin as the outcome of atonement — the cultic and covenantal restoration of the person's standing before God through sacrifice and priestly mediation
Term names the intercession for divine forgiveness — the act of seeking God's pardon on behalf of oneself or others, and God's response of pardoning or withholding
Term names God's promised and proclaimed forgiveness in the new covenant context — the unconditional covenantal act by which God forgives iniquity and remembers sin no more
Term names forgiveness as a characteristic attribute of God's inner being — the disposition of readiness to pardon that belongs to his essential goodness and steadfast love

Term names forgiveness of sins as the spiritual transaction through which the inner person is released from guilt and restored to right standing before God — received through repentance, faith, and Christ's atoning blood
Term names the absolute limit of forgiveness — the unforgivable sin against the Holy Spirit, and the conditions under which forgiveness is withheld or inaccessible

Term names forgiveness as a divine attribute and possession — a quality that belongs to God's character, that grounds the possibility of the human-divine relationship, and that evokes reverential fear
Term names God's gracious giving and forgiving — the divine act of freely granting, releasing, and bestowing on the basis of grace alone, supremely in the gift of the Son and forgiveness of sins

Term names the human act of forgiving — releasing another from debt, offence, or resentment, patterned on and grounded in the divine forgiveness received, as an inner-relational act of grace extended to another
Term names the act of bestowing grace — the divine favour poured upon a person or people as a relational act that marks and transforms the one so favoured

Term names grace as the sovereign, unmerited divine disposition toward humanity — the freely given, transforming gift through which God justifies, saves, calls, and sustains, supremely displayed in Christ

Term names grace as the relational sphere and power in which the believer stands and lives — the ongoing inner-relational condition of being under grace, accessed by faith, sustained by God, and expressed in the transformed inner being
Term names grace as relational favour — the disposition of goodwill between persons or between a person and God, which opens access, creates loyalty, and reflects the character of the one granting it

Term names grace as the animating power and sphere of apostolic mission — the word of grace, the commendation to grace, the extending of grace to more people, and the inner source of ministry identity

Term names the divine relational favour bestowed on a person — the inner-relational disposition of God toward those he knows by name, which creates standing, protection, and access, and which is sought, recognised, and treasured
Term names relational favour between persons — the inner-relational disposition of goodwill one person seeks and receives from another, which creates access, loyalty, and life
Term names favour as inner character quality — the gracious disposition expressed in speech, conduct, and wisdom that generates standing and blessing in community
Term names the inner act of earnest supplication — the plea for mercy that arises from deep need and relational vulnerability before God, offered with the expectation that God hears and responds
Kindness as inner quality of love — outward expression of love's inner character toward others
Deceptive inner disposition expressed as flattering speech — false kindness targeting the naive
Departure from relational presence expressing inner relational states — grief at loss, guilt, inner fear of separation
Military force as communal subject of inner responses to battle — fear, courage, trust in God, inner orientation required at war
Relational community of belonging — gathered to or cut off from one's people as inner belonging, covenant faithfulness, or moral transgression
Relational presence of God or companions as context for inner experience — worship, petition, strengthening, or humbling
Covenant community as object of God's relational love and moral claim — 'my people' as relational partner of the divine
Corporate inner spiritual condition of the people — rebellion, fear, trust, shame, orientation toward God
People as bearer of communal inner identity — shared moral-spiritual character defining who 'the people' are
Covenant community as object of God's relational love and moral claim — 'my people' as relational partner of the divine
Corporate inner spiritual condition of the people — rebellion, fear, trust, shame, orientation toward God
People as bearer of communal inner identity — shared moral-spiritual character defining who 'the people' are
Covenant community as object of God's relational love and moral claim — 'my people' as relational partner of the divine
Corporate inner spiritual condition of the people — rebellion, fear, trust, shame, orientation toward God
People as bearer of communal inner identity — shared moral-spiritual character defining who 'the people' are
Term connects and qualifies inner-being content — directing attention to persons or conditions where inner life is expressed
Term frames inner reflection on meaning and worth of human experience — wisdom on joy, futility, divine gifting
Term directs and intensifies expression of inner pride — pointing to object of self-glorification
Term directs attention to consequence of inner moral orientation — outcome of covenant faithfulness or spiritual infidelity
Term directs attention to inner-being quality, state, act, or object — identifying or intensifying inner-being engagement
Term frames divine presence as the condition of inner trust, security, and confident orientation toward God
Term marks the zone of direct divine encounter with the self — whether affliction or care — as shaping the person's inner state
Term frames a relational/covenantal bond expressing inner orientation of faithfulness, loyalty, or dependence
Inner moral condition characteristic of humanity — evil inclination of the heart, capacity for wickedness
Human frailty and dependence before God — inner limitation and creaturely orientation defining adam
Humanity as object of God's purposeful action — created in God's image, known, called to reflect divine character
Specific inner dispositions of individual persons as representative human qualities — meekness, wisdom, contrition, humility
Goodness/kindness as inner quality of God or persons — disposition of gracious favour expressed toward others
Term names friendship as a relational bond grounded in inner knowledge, mutual self-giving, and shared life with Christ — and exposes the contrast with friendship with the world as enmity with God
Term names the kiss as the outward expression of an inner relational reality — betrayal, absent love exposed, or holy community bond
Term names the kiss as physical expression of an inner state — love and grief, contrition and gratitude, betrayal, or joyful reunion
Term names friendship with the world as a disordered inner orientation constituting enmity with God — the inward allegiance that cannot serve two masters
Term names the loyal friend whose inner orientation of faithfulness transcends social convention — set against flattery, abandonment, and betrayal
Term names the beloved as the object of intense longing, delight, and exclusive devotion — the relational centre of the Song's inner world of love
Term names "sister" as relational identity grounded in spiritual orientation — redefining family on the basis of obedience to God and mutual moral care
Term names the brotherhood of believers as a shared inner-being reality — of love to be directed toward it and suffering to be borne within it
Term names the false brother as one whose inner orientation is concealed deception — appearing to share the community bond while working to undermine its freedom
Term names God's compassion as a defining inner attribute — the divine disposition of tender mercy toward the afflicted and repentant
Term names the neighbour as the moral category defining the inner person's obligation — the one toward whom honesty, care, justice, and love are required as inner character
Term names the companion as the one to whom loyalty is shown or from whom it is withheld — the relational bond whose presence or absence discloses inner character
Term names the trusted friend whose inner loyalty shapes courageous action — Hushai's friendship with David as the ground of his protective deception
Term names the companion in communal grief — the friends who accompany another in their inner lamentation
Term names the peacemaker as one whose inner orientation produces peace — a beatitude character quality
Term names the inner disposition of hatred toward another — used in the context of wishing that judgment falls on those who bear it
Term names Christ as firstborn — carrying theological weight about his identity, primacy, and the inner-being significance of belonging to his redeemed community
Term names the birthright as sacred inheritance — whose careless disposal reveals inner contempt for what is holy, a warning about Esau's moral failure
Term names the love-relationship in its full range — from covenantal God-Israel love and the Song's mutual delight to adulterous love as the distortion of the same inner longing
Term names the beloved as the object of God's deepest attachment and delight — used for persons and Israel in election; its betrayal marking covenant grief
Term names Israel as the beloved of God's soul — the most intimate inner designation, whose abandonment into enemy hands marks God's deepest grief
Term names God's steadfast love as his defining inner attribute — the covenantal faithfulness that never ceases, extends to heaven, and is the ground of Israel's hope and petition
Term names human expressions of covenantal loyalty and kindness — the inner disposition of faithfulness between persons that mirrors divine chesed
Term names the failure or withdrawal of chesed — its absence as the condition of moral and spiritual ruin; also the passionate demand for it as the priority above ritual
Term names God's love for his people — the covenantal inner disposition of steadfast love that persists through and exceeds all human response
Term names the intense mutual love between persons — including Jonathan's love for David and the burning love of the Song
Term names love as moral loyalty and kindness — the inner orientation of faithfulness between persons that mirrors divine chesed
Term names the lover — either as the rightful object of marital delight, or the hired substitute marking spiritual infidelity
Term names the godly or pious person — one whose inner character is shaped by covenantal faithfulness toward God, set apart for his purposes and preserved by him
Term names the state of inner wellbeing, pleasure, or moral approval — the heart merry or satisfied, God pleased with what is in the heart, the person judging something good
Term names God's compassion as the deep inner movement of mercy — his mother-like tenderness toward those who suffer, his readiness to forgive and restore
Term names the darling or beloved as the cherished object of delight — the intimate address of love in the Song
Term names what is fitting, lovely, or becoming — expressing the inner sense of rightness and beauty that governs aesthetic delight and moral appropriateness
Term names the inner disposition of lust — the corrupted desire that drives spiritual adultery and moral ruin
Term names the pleasant or sweet quality of what delights the inner being — communion, wisdom, worship, and the presence of the beloved as experiences of deep inner pleasantness

Term names hatred as the inner moral disposition opposed to love — God's righteous hatred of evil and injustice; human hatred of neighbour and brother as the mark of moral disorder; and the psalmist's holy hatred of evil aligned with God
Term names the divine act of compassion — God's inner movement of mercy and pity toward his people, especially in restoration after judgment
Term names God-loving as the defining inner orientation — the standard from which the last days' pleasure-loving represents the ultimate departure
Term names tender, family-like affection as the inner disposition of believers toward one another
Term names Christ as the Father's beloved — the unique inner-relationship of love between Father and Son expressed in the divine declaration at baptism and transfiguration
Term names the believer as beloved — addressed as the cherished object of God's and the community's love, called to live and act from that identity
Term names God's loving kindness toward humanity as an inner divine disposition made manifest — the benevolent character of God revealed in the Saviour's appearing
Term names brotherly love as the inner-relational disposition that binds the community of faith — to be cultivated, continued, and grounded in purified hearts
Term names love of one's children as an inner affective disposition to be cultivated — part of the domestic virtue formation of women in the community
Term names the contentious inner disposition — the inner inclination toward argument that Paul refuses to validate as a community practice
Term names love of one's husband as an inner affective orientation to be cultivated and trained — a domestic virtue shaped by inner formation
Term names God's love as the inner source and ground of all love — poured into hearts, demonstrated in Christ's death, inseparable from the believer
Term names love as the defining inner virtue of the believer — the fruit of the Spirit, the greatest commandment, the fulfilment of the law, that which never ends
Term names mutual love among believers as the communal inner orientation — the mark of discipleship, the bond of the community
Term names love of dispute as an inner orientation toward rivalry and status — the disciples' competitive inner state at the Last Supper
Term names love of money as a disordered inner orientation — a root of all kinds of evils that corrupts faith and pierces the person with suffering
Term names loveliness as a quality that merits and directs the inner attention of the mind in moral reflection
Term names love of good as an inner character disposition — required of those who lead the community of faith
Term names love of brothers as an inner disposition among the virtues of the unified community
Term names love of money as an inner character trait — a disposition marking the Pharisee's distorted orientation and the last days' catalogue of inner failures
Term names freedom from love of money as an inner character quality — required of leaders and urged for all believers
Term names love of pleasure as a disordered inner orientation — set in direct contrast to love of God as the mark of the last days' inner collapse
Term names love of God or Christ as the fundamental inner orientation of the person — the love commanded of the whole heart, soul, mind, and strength
Term names God's or Christ's love toward the person — the divine inner act of love that precedes and enables all human love
Term names love of neighbour and enemy — the inner-being orientation outward that fulfils the law
Term names mutual love among believers and within marriage — the inner relational orientation that marks discipleship and covenant
Term names disordered love — love directed toward the world, darkness, money, or self-glory as the defining inner orientation of the fallen or departing person
Term names natural affection or friendship-love between persons — including Christ's love for his friends and the relational love of community
Term names the ranking of inner loves and loyalties — the cost of placing love of Christ above natural affection, and the exposure of disordered love of self or world
Term names Peter's confession of natural love — the Joh 21 dialogue where fileo names the warmth Peter can affirm when agapao feels too great a claim
Term names the love of being first as an inner character disposition — the drive for primacy that refuses to acknowledge legitimate authority
Term names compassion as the inner character of God and the standard for human inner disposition — to be merciful as God is merciful
Term names mercifulness as an inner character quality — the blessed disposition of those who show mercy and the defining character of Christ as high priest
Term names mercilessness as an inner character failure — one of the marks of those whose inner moral fabric has collapsed
Term names the propitious disposition of God — the mercy that forgives iniquity and remembers sin no more under the new covenant
Term names the priestly/ritual act by which human guilt is covered before God and forgiveness granted — atonement as the mechanism addressing the person's moral condition
Term names God's direct act of forgiving and covering iniquity — atonement as divine grace beyond or fulfilling the ritual mechanism
Term names interpersonal or communal appeasement — covering relational offence or bloodguilt between persons or before God on communal grounds
Term names a substitutionary price paid for a life — ransom as the means by which moral accountability for life is addressed before God or human authority
Term names illicit payment that corrupts the inner moral vision of the recipient — a bribe that blinds or deflects justice
Term names the formal atonement institution — the Day of Atonement and its offerings — as the sacred structure addressing Israel's collective moral condition before God
Term names the priestly/ritual act by which human guilt is covered before God and forgiveness granted
Term names God's direct act of forgiving and covering iniquity
Term names interpersonal or communal appeasement — covering relational offence or bloodguilt
Term names the act of propitiation — the sinner's inner cry for God to turn his face in mercy, and Christ's atoning act that makes this possible
Term names the propitiation — Christ as the atoning covering for sin that restores the inner relationship between God and the person
Term names unholiness as an inner character disposition — among the catalogue of inner moral failures marking those outside covenant fidelity
Term names devout holiness as an inner character quality — the holy conduct of the apostle as a witness to inner moral integrity
Term names the act of divine mercy — God having mercy on whom he wills as the ground of salvation and sovereign grace
Term names the cry for mercy — the supplicant's inner anguish and hope directed toward Jesus as healer and Saviour
Term names the human act of mercy as the inner disposition that receives mercy in return
Term names the mercy seat as the physical locus of divine-human encounter — the point where God's presence meets human representation and atonement is effected
Term names God's mercy as his defining inner attribute — the ground of salvation, the content of covenant faithfulness, and the wellspring of new life
Term names mercy as the inner-being virtue and judicial reality — what God desires above ritual, what the wise show, what triumphs over judgment
Term names the act of being gracious or showing favour — God's inner disposition of grace toward the supplicant, and the human cry that turns to God in petition and dependence
Term names supplication to God — the inner act of pleading for mercy, rooted in recognition of one's own need and God's disposition to hear and forgive
Term names supplication to human authority — the humble inner posture of plea before one with power over the supplicant's life or freedom
Term names the prophetic withdrawal of divine compassion — the name embodies God's inner-being relational stance of withheld mercy toward a covenant-breaking people
Term names the act of intercessory prayer — pleading with God on behalf of another's need or the removal of a burden from others
Term names personal prayer — the inner act of bringing one's own need, trust, or longing to God and receiving divine response
Term names divine compassion as the ground of appeal — the inner-being disposition of God toward persons in extremity
Term names mercy shown in moral action and the plea of prayer — the practice of mercy toward the oppressed and the inner posture of petition before God
Term intensifies or marks the persistence/cessation of a human inner-being state — amplifying the force of an inner disposition or qualifying the duration of inner orientation
Term marks the ongoing or continuing character of a divine inner disposition — God's sustained relational stance toward a people
Term names the mercy seat — the place of propitiation fulfilled in Christ as the living propitiation received by faith
Term names the compassions of God as his defining inner character — the mercies that motivate holy living and compassionate hearts as the inner garment of the new humanity
Term names mercilessness as the inner orientation that forfeits mercy — and mercy as the disposition that triumphs over judgment
Term names a signal or banner that orients the inner life — the object to which hope, fear, or trust is directed, or from which the inner life flees in panic
Term names the relational orientation of Israel toward God — the shift from servile master to intimate husband naming the transformation of inner relationship
Term names a disposition of quiet attentiveness or ordered restraint — the inner quality of settled quiet that enables learning, work, or worship
Term names a gentle, quiet spirit as an inner-being quality — the disposition of settled peace within, precious in God's sight
Term names profound silence as an inner-being atmosphere — the hush of awe before a significant moment
Term names the inner condition of ease and security — the state of the person wholly undisturbed, here in the context of unjust prosperity
Term names quietness as a condition of divinely bestowed peace — the character of a reign and its people under God's gift of rest
Term names the soothing/pleasing quality of sacrifice as a relational act — the inner-being dimension of worship as something God receives with pleasure, establishing a condition of divine acceptance
Term names God's inner response to the aroma — the explicit disclosure of God's heart orientation through the receiving or withholding of acceptance
Term names the misdirected inner-being act of worship — the offering of pleasing aroma to idols as an expression of unfaithful inner orientation
Term names the soul's own rest — the inner condition of peace, security, and settled orientation toward God
Term names the covenantal rest given by God to his people — a condition of settled security, peace, and inheritance promised and fulfilled
Term names an eschatological or divine resting-place — where God dwells, and the orientation of his own will and delight
Term names a state of existential restlessness — the absence of resting-place as the condition of the whole person, particularly in exile or distress
Term names quietness as an inner-being condition of peace, receptivity to wisdom, and strength before God
Term names the granting of relief — a remission that brings rest and lightening of burden to those who receive it
Term names God's disturbing/stirring power in creation — an expression of divine inner capacity and sovereignty
Term names rest as the condition of the soul that walks in the right way — an inner-being orientation of peace attained through faithful obedience
Term names divine repose offered to the weary — the condition of inner rest available through God's provision
Term names the restful inner posture of the peaceable person — the quality of quietness as a character orientation, in contrast to those who devise deceit
Term names the inner act of becoming or keeping silent — the stilling of the self before God, in grief, in reverence, or in wise restraint
Term names the inner state of stillness or its absence — being motionless before God in awe, patient waiting, or unceasing distress
Term names the cessation or ruination of the inner person — the collapse of human dignity, prophetic undoing before holiness, or unceasing grief
Term names the inner disposition of hushed stillness — silence as judgment's response or as the posture of patient waiting for divine salvation
Term names the stillness from which the divine voice is heard — the profound inner and outer silence that attends divine self-disclosure
Term names the posture of inner restraint — holding silence in response to emotional provocation, injustice, or the desire to speak
Term names the receptive posture of silence — an inner orientation of attentive submission, readiness to hear or receive
Term names the posture of wisdom — silence as an expression of inner discernment, understanding, or moral restraint
Term names the inner condition when an appeal is made for God not to be silent — the psalmist's existential need for divine responsiveness
Term names God's own posture of silence or its breaking — divine inner restraint, patience, or deliberate withholding
Term applied to ethical-existential confrontation — the moral weight of remaining silent at a critical moment of injustice or crisis
Term names the inward suppression of speech under emotional or spiritual pressure, with deteriorating inner consequence
Term names the moral dimension of silence — the choice to be silent or not at a moment of ethical weight
Term names God's posture of silence and its breaking — divine restraint, patience, or resolve
Term names prophetic inner resolve — refusing silence in advocacy for God's purposes
Term names divine blessing as the inner-dispositioned gift of God — the overflow of God's favour toward persons, bringing flourishing, peace, and life
Term names the inner act of bestowing blessing — the patriarch, leader, or friend invoking divine favour upon another as an act of love, covenant, or relationship
Term names the inner condition of complacent ease — the self-secure, untroubled disposition that produces contempt, moral blindness, or judgment
Term names the divinely intended condition of secure quietness — the ease of God's people living in peace and safety as a condition of shalom
Term names the formal salutation of peace — the invocation of wholeness and wellbeing at the opening of official correspondence
Term names the inner devising of evil — the heart plotting harm or crafting schemes against another
Term names the metaphorical plowing of iniquity — inner sin etched deeply into the heart or expressed as a pattern of moral cultivation
Term names the inner condition of quietness, rest, and trust as the spiritual posture of the person before God — the condition from which strength and salvation flow
Term names the inner restlessness of the wicked or troubled person — the inability to be quiet as an expression of inner moral or emotional disorder
Term names the quiet of God — God's own inner posture of watchful stillness, or the breaking of it in response to his people's need or his own resolve
Term names the promised rest of God's people — the condition of quiet and ease as covenant blessing, contrasted with restlessness under judgment
Term names the Spirit resting upon a person — divine inner-being impartation resulting in transformed capacities
Term names God's gift of rest to his people — the settled condition of security, peace, and wholeness bestowed as covenant blessing
Term names the inner restlessness or rest of the human person — the condition of the inner life in the absence or presence of peace
Term names wisdom, anger, or another inner quality resting in a place within the person
Term names God's own inner rest, restraint, satisfaction, or calm — the inner orientation of God expressed through this root
Term names the call or appeal not to be abandoned by God — the inner-being distress of one fearing divine withdrawal
Term names the rest or respite sought or denied — the inner-being condition of peace in exile, distress, or covenant promise
Term names the commanded inner silence — the hush of reverence, awe, or holy restraint before God or in the face of his coming
Term names the inner disposition and active practice of relational peace — the will directed toward harmony with others
Term names the inner state and act of silence — holding the voice still in the presence of power, shame, or reverence
Term names the inner disposition of settling into quiet — the ceasing of noise, argument, or resistance, whether in reverence, acceptance, or godly aspiration
Term names the inner discipline of silence — the restraint of voice in reverence, receptiveness, or ordered worship
Term names the inner-being peace given by God through Christ — the reconciled state before God and the interior experience of divine peace guarding the heart and mind
Term names peace as apostolic blessing and greeting — the invocation of divine shalom upon persons in the community of Christ
Term names peace as the eschatological and relational fruit of the kingdom — achieved by Christ, to be pursued in community and received as the Spirit's gift
Term names peaceableness as an inner character quality — the disposition of the trained person or the wisdom from above, producing righteous fruit
Term names peaceful quietude as an inner-being quality of life — the disposition of undisturbed, godly calm in social context
Term names relief from pressure or affliction — the inner-being experience of rest when burdens are lifted, or its absence when they persist
Term names the act of making peace — Christ's reconciling work that restores the broken relationship between humanity and God
Term names the silencing of voice or argument — the constraint of expression, whether as shame, spiritual authority, or the witness of righteous action
Term names the jealousy of God as a defining inner characteristic — God's exclusive passionate claim on his people's worship and loyalty
Term names the dwelling of God's presence as an inner-oriented rest-place, where his people find salvation and joy
Term names relief as a condition of the inner life freed from threat
Term names reconciliation as the inner-relational restoration of broken relationship between God and humanity — the ending of enmity and establishment of peace at the relational core of personhood
Term names the act of reconciliation — the active work of restoring broken inner-relational standing, whether between God and humanity or within human relationships
